vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current physical state. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not clearly distinguished in the shared black pot. P3 (Pothos) is present but severely diminished.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2/P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint or silver guest foliage; only soil and debris.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Degraded: Only a single, thin, chlorotic stem remains visible in the black pot with the rabbit anchor.

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains soil and white mineral/shell debris; no viable plant matter.
plant_audit.P3: Single thin green stem remains; lack of leaf mass indicates severe physiological stress.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of white crushed material (likely eggshells or mineral supplement) in the P3/P4 area.
biome_observations.surface_growth: No moss or secondary sprouts detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a rapid decline. The initial image shows a small green sprout in the black pot, which has remained static and failed to develop, while other pots have remained barren throughout the 5-day observation window.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of photosynthetic tissue suggests the plants have either succumbed to root rot (due to constant dampness) or extreme light deficiency. The single remaining stem in P3 is the only evidence of life.
vision_report.anomalies: The white crushed material is a clear 'New Intervention' not present in the baseline registry. The lack of growth in the 'Mexican Mint' pot suggests a failure of the initial planting or total mortality.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently non-viable. Over the 5-day observation period, there has been zero positive growth. The 'String of Nickels' and 'Mexican Mint' are effectively lost. The Pothos (P3) is clinging to life with a single, fragile stem. The presence of white debris suggests an attempt at soil amendment that has not yielded any biological recovery.
